_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to measure application whitelisting/blacklisting solutions
reference_document_element_description: Implement model monitoring and observability systems that detect data drift, prediction drift, and performance degradation, triggering alerts or automated retraining workflows when defined thresholds are exceeded.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-T-017 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-005 (RC, cosine: 0.356), DQ-T-016 (DQ, cosine: 0.348).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.5/10 (raw 0.451). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [ability, application, blacklisting, measure, solutions, whitelisting] vs WIDAI-only terms [alerts, automated, data, defined, degradation, detect] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.843 (P=0.851, R=0.835). Balanced P/R — the 6-word DCWF element and 26-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Ability to measure application whitelisting/blacklisting solutions" and "Implement model monitoring and observability systems that detect data drift, pre" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.358 < STS 0.451 (gap -0.093). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [ability, application, blacklisting] and [alerts, automated, data] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [ability, application, blacklisting, measure, solutions, whitelisting]. WIDAI terms: [alerts, automated, data, defined, degradation, detect].
